text.title: role-specialized-models Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for role-specialized-models. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Pin the strongest model to planning and final review; pin the mid model to execution; pin the smallest model to classification, slot-filling, and reformatting.
rule.rationale: Anthropic's "advisor strategy" and Claude Code subagent guides report ~3x cost reduction at unchanged quality on long-running coding tasks; the small token share of planning means the strong-model premium is paid on a small slice while preserving its leverage.

rule.statement: For each role, run a 30-prompt eval against the next-cheaper model and the next-stronger model; promote down a tier if quality holds within 1 pp, promote up a tier if quality falls more than 3 pp.
rule.rationale: The Pareto sweet spot drifts as model versions ship; locking a role to a model on launch leaves cost on the table within months. Periodic ablation keeps the assignment honest.

rule.statement: Each role hand-off must be a structured artifact (JSON plan, list of file paths, list of acceptance criteria) — never a verbatim copy of the prior conversation; the next role consumes the artifact, not the chat.
rule.rationale: Conversation history pollutes the cheaper model with strong-model reasoning it cannot reproduce, and it bloats the stronger model when it returns to review. Artifacts keep each role's input minimal and its output verifiable.

rule.statement: After every transition from a stronger to a weaker model, the stronger model must verify the artifact returned by the weaker one against the original acceptance criteria before any further action.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit gate, errors compound across roles: a misclassified file path in the small model corrupts the entire execution step. The verification is cheap (the strong model only reads a small artifact) and prevents expensive cascades.
